name: one-sided Lipschitz condition
statement_text:
The function f satisfies a ‘one-sided Lipschitz condition’,
with ‘one-sided Lipschitz constant’ l if for all x ∈ [a, b] and all u, v ∈ RN ,
                        f (x, u) − f (x, v), u − v ≤ l u − v 2 .
It is possible that the function f could have a very large Lipschitz constant
but a moderately sized, or even negative, one-sided Lipschitz constant. The
advantage of this is seen in the following result.
statement_latex:
\begin{definition}
The function $f$ satisfies a `one-sided Lipschitz condition',
with `one-sided Lipschitz constant' $l$ if for all $x \in [a, b]$ and all $u, v \in \mathbb{R}^N$,
\[
\langle f(x, u) - f(x, v), u - v \rangle \leq l \| u - v \|^2.
\]
It is possible that the function $f$ could have a very large Lipschitz constant
but a moderately sized, or even negative, one-sided Lipschitz constant. The
advantage of this is seen in the following result.
\end{definition}

variables.name: f
variables.definition: f : [a, b] × R^N → R^N, the function defining the differential equation system

variables.name: a, b
variables.definition: endpoints of the interval [a, b] on which the differential equation is posed

variables.name: N
variables.definition: dimension of the space R^N, where the solution and function values lie

variables.name: ⟨u, v⟩
variables.definition: inner product on R^N, with induced norm ‖u‖^2 = ⟨u, u⟩

preamble: The context considers differential equation systems posed on an inner product space R^N, with inner product ⟨·,·⟩ and norm defined from it. The function f defines the system, and the one-sided Lipschitz condition is introduced to analyze stiff problems.

dependencies.name: Lipschitz condition in its second variable
